description: Establish Kubernetes as the compute platform for The Data Platform by creating a Pulumi-based infrastructure layer that provisions clusters for both local development (k3d) and production (k3s on Linode). Three-layer architecture: cluster provisioning, platform bootstrap, and application deployment.

userStories.title: Initialize Pulumi cluster project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Pulumi project for cluster provisioning so that I can create Kubernetes clusters via infrastructure as code.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/cluster/Pulumi.yaml exists with project name tdp-cluster and runtime nodejs
monorepo/infra/cluster/package.json exists with @pulumi/pulumi and @pulumi/command dependencies
monorepo/infra/cluster/tsconfig.json exists with strict TypeScript config
monorepo/infra/cluster/Pulumi.local.yaml exists with clusterType: k3d and clusterName: tdp-local
monorepo/infra/cluster/index.ts exists and dispatches to provider modules based on clusterType config value
npm install succeeds in the cluster directory
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Effort: M. This is the foundation — all other infra tasks depend on this.

userStories.title: Implement k3d cluster provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a k3d cluster provider so that I can spin up a local Kubernetes cluster with a container registry for development.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/cluster/providers/k3d.ts exists and exports a function or class that creates a k3d cluster
Uses @pulumi/command local.Command to run k3d CLI commands
Creates a local registry via k3d registry create
Configures port mappings for ports 80 and 443 (for Traefik ingress)
Supports configurable worker node count via Pulumi config (default: 2)
Exports kubeconfig as a Pulumi output
Includes destroy/cleanup commands for the cluster and registry
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)

userStories.title: Initialize Pulumi platform project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Pulumi project for platform bootstrapping so that core services can be installed on any cluster via a stack reference to the cluster layer.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/platform/Pulumi.yaml exists with project name tdp-platform and runtime nodejs
monorepo/infra/platform/package.json exists with @pulumi/pulumi and @pulumi/kubernetes dependencies
monorepo/infra/platform/tsconfig.json exists with strict TypeScript config
monorepo/infra/platform/Pulumi.local.yaml exists with stack config
monorepo/infra/platform/index.ts exists and uses a StackReference to read kubeconfig from the cluster project
Creates a k8s.Provider using the referenced kubeconfig
npm install succeeds in the platform directory
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)

userStories.title: Verify Traefik ingress and configure platform layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the platform layer to verify Traefik is running and install additional platform charts (cert-manager, monitoring) so that the cluster has core services available.
acceptanceCriteria: Platform index.ts includes a check or note that Traefik is bundled with k3s/k3d and does not install a separate ingress controller
monorepo/infra/platform/charts/cert-manager.ts exists and installs cert-manager via Helm chart with installCRDs: true
monorepo/infra/platform/charts/monitoring.ts exists and installs kube-prometheus-stack via Helm chart with environment-appropriate resource limits
Platform index.ts imports and uses the chart modules
Documentation exists (comments or README) on how to define Ingress resources that work with Traefik
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-002, US-003.

userStories.title: Create ServiceDeployment component resource
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable Pulumi ComponentResource that encapsulates a Kubernetes Deployment + Service + optional Ingress so that application deployments are consistent across services.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/components/package.json exists with @pulumi/pulumi and @pulumi/kubernetes dependencies
monorepo/infra/components/tsconfig.json exists
monorepo/infra/components/service-deployment.ts exports a ServiceDeployment class extending pulumi.ComponentResource
ServiceDeployment accepts parameters: image, port, replicas, env, resources, ingress (host, tls)
Applies consistent app.kubernetes.io/* labels (name, part-of: the-data-platform, managed-by: pulumi)
Creates a Deployment, Service, and optionally an Ingress resource
monorepo/infra/components/index.ts re-exports the component
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)

userStories.title: Create local-up and local-down scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shell scripts that spin up and tear down the full local Kubernetes environment so that I can get started with a single command.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/scripts/local-up.sh exists and is executable
local-up.sh validates prerequisites (docker, k3d, pulumi, node/npm) and exits with a helpful error if any are missing
local-up.sh runs pulumi up for the cluster project (local stack), then the platform project (local stack)
local-up.sh prints cluster access info on success (kubeconfig path, registry URL, how to set KUBECONFIG)
monorepo/infra/scripts/local-down.sh exists and is executable
local-down.sh runs pulumi destroy for platform (local stack), then cluster (local stack)
Both scripts use set -euo pipefail for safety
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-001, US-002, US-003.

userStories.title: Add smoke test for local cluster
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a smoke test that deploys a hello-world pod and verifies it's reachable via ingress so that I can confirm the local cluster is working end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: A smoke test script or Pulumi project exists that deploys a minimal container using the ServiceDeployment component
The deployment is accessible via Traefik ingress on localhost
The test verifies HTTP connectivity to the deployed service
The test cleans up after itself (removes the test deployment)
Can be run manually via a script (e.g., monorepo/infra/scripts/smoke-test.sh)
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-005, US-006. Human review: end-to-end verification checkpoint.

userStories.title: Implement Linode k3s cluster provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Linode k3s cluster provider so that I can provision a production Kubernetes cluster on Linode using the same Pulumi infrastructure code.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/cluster/providers/linode-k3s.ts exists and exports a function or class that provisions a Linode instance with k3s
Uses @pulumi/linode to create a Linode 2GB instance
Uses @pulumi/command remote provisioner to install k3s via curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -
Configures Linode firewall rules to allow ports 80, 443, and 6443 (K8s API)
Exports kubeconfig as a Pulumi output
monorepo/infra/cluster/Pulumi.production.yaml exists with clusterType: linode-k3s config
package.json includes @pulumi/linode dependency
index.ts dispatches to linode-k3s provider when clusterType is linode-k3s
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Depends on US-001. Human review: production infrastructure requires verification before use.

userStories.title: Create base Dockerfile and example service image
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a base Dockerfile for Python services so that I can build container images and load them into the local k3d registry.
acceptanceCriteria: A base Dockerfile for Python services exists (e.g., monorepo/services/Dockerfile.base or similar)
An example Dockerfile exists demonstrating how to build a service image
The example image builds successfully with docker build
The image can be loaded into the k3d registry (via k3d image import or pushing to the k3d registry)
A brief README or comments document the image build + push workflow for local development
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-002 for k3d registry testing.

userStories.title: Document local development workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear documentation on how to use the local Kubernetes environment so that I can onboard quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: monorepo/infra/README.md is updated with comprehensive local development docs
Documents prerequisites (Docker, k3d, Pulumi, Node.js, kubectl)
Documents quick start workflow (./scripts/local-up.sh)
Documents how to deploy a service to the local cluster
Documents how to access services (ingress URLs, port-forwarding)
Documents teardown (./scripts/local-down.sh)
All referenced scripts and paths are accurate
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Final task — human review to verify docs are accurate and complete.
